methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Valthos is a Seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Kathleen McMahon and Tess van Stekelenburg that has raised $30M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 5 private peers at the same or adjacent stage, including Tenna Systems ($60M), Ares Industries ($150M), NODA AI ($125M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Stark Defence ($500M), alongside broader reference to 8 private companies and 2 public entities in the sector.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $30M raised as a proxy for current value and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from Seed to the next stage, implies a valuation of $75M. Peer-median analysis across the 5 comparable companies yields a median valuation of $150M, with the peer range spanning $60M to $500M. These methods reflect both the company's current capital position and its relative standing within a cohort of similarly-staged Defense & Security firms.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $64M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $112M. This range reflects material uncertainty around the company's technology readiness, go-to-market execution relative to peers, and customer traction—dimensions not captured in stage and peer metrics alone. The $108M spread between low and high estimates underscores the importance of commercial validation in narrowing the valuation range at this stage.
